name: OpenAI Assistant with custom n8n tools
description: Automated workflow: OpenAI Assistant with custom n8n tools. This workflow integrates 12 different services: stickyNote, openAiAssistant, code, merge, summarize. It contains 15 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: OpenAI Assistant with custom n8n tools workflow containing 15 nodes.

parameters.content:
## Sub-workflow: Return the capitals of fictional countries
It can either list the countries it knows about or return the capital of a specific country

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Return country list
nodes.notes: This set node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Return specific capital
nodes.notes: This set node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Tool to call the workflow below

parameters.name: country_capitals_tool
parameters.description:
This tool has two modes:
1. Pass 'list' to the tool to get a list of countries that the tool has the capitals for (one per line). This is useful if you can't find a match, to see if the country being asked about might have been misspelled.
2. Pass one of the country names in the list to the tool to get the capital of that country. Note that the country must be spelled exactly as it is in the list of countries returned in mode 1

nodes.name: Tool to call the workflow below

parameters.description:
This tool has two modes:
1. Pass 'list' to the tool to get a list of countries that the tool has the capitals for (one per line). This is useful if you can't find a match, to see if the country being asked about might have been misspelled.
2. Pass one of the country names in the list to the tool to get the capital of that country. Note that the country must be spelled exactly as it is in the list of countries returned in mode 1

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Execute Workflow Trigger".
Open the "On new manual Chat Message" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Assistant" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "List countries?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Tool to call the workflow below" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Concatenate country names" node (n8n-nodes-base.summarize) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get the matching country's details" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Tool: Get current date and time" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Return country list".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Return specific capital".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
